title: Collection Persistence, Versioned Schemas, Atomic Replacement, and Restore · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds versioned atomic offline collection store from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned atomic offline collection store while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Keep browser lessons on pure byte models and place native filesystem effects behind an injected save and load port.
Define version, application identity, timestamp policy, records, provenance, and checksum or integrity scope with deterministic encoding.
Bound bytes, decode exactly one document, validate schema and every record, reject duplicates, and preserve the last valid in-memory state on error.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Persistence Bo…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 6853 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate versioned collection bytes and prove failure-safe atomic replacement and restore; the release claim must identify the exact source and artifact revision; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep browser lessons on pure byte models and place native filesystem effects behind an injected save and load port.

What must be established before implementation?

Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-migration-restore-tests-1tzc67" that uses case 9388 to validate versioned collection bytes and prove failure-safe atomic replacement and restore. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.
